info.title: Resend
info.description: Resend is the email platform for developers.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A unique identifier for the request to ensure emails are only sent once. [Learn more](https://resend.com/docs/dashboard/emails/idempotency-keys)

post.summary: Cancel the schedule of the e-mail.
Trigger up to 100 batch emails at once.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A unique identifier for the request to ensure emails are only sent once. [Learn more](https://resend.com/docs/dashboard/emails/idempotency-keys)

get.summary: Retrieve a list of attachments for a sent email

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of attachments to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results after this attachment ID. Cannot be used with 'before'.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results before this attachment ID. Cannot be used with 'after'.

get.summary: Retrieve a single attachment for a sent email
Retrieve a list of received emails

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of received emails to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results after this email ID. Cannot be used with 'before'.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results before this email ID. Cannot be used with 'after'.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: The ID of the received email.

get.summary: Retrieve a list of attachments for a received email

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: The ID of the received email.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of attachments to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results after this attachment ID. Cannot be used with 'before'.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results before this attachment ID. Cannot be used with 'after'.

get.summary: Retrieve a single attachment for a received email

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: The ID of the received email.

post.description: Triggers verification of the domain's DNS records including DKIM, SPF, and the tracking CNAME (if a tracking subdomain is configured).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: "Filter runs by status. Comma-separated list of: running, completed, failed, cancelled."

parameters.description: The ID of the automation run.

parameters.description: The event ID (UUID) or event name.

from.description: Sender email address. To include a friendly name, use the format "Your Name <sender@domain.com>".

to.description: Recipient email address. For multiple addresses, send as an array of strings. Max 50.

bcc.description: Bcc recipient email address. For multiple addresses, send as an array of strings.

cc.description: Cc recipient email address. For multiple addresses, send as an array of strings.

reply_to.description: Reply-to email address. For multiple addresses, send as an array of strings.

html.description: The HTML version of the message.

text.description: The plain text version of the message.

allOf.description: Use a published template to send the email. If provided, do not include html or text.

id.description: The id of the published email template.

variables.description: Template variables object with key/value pairs.

headers.description: Custom headers to add to the email.

scheduled_at.description: Schedule email to be sent later. The date should be in ISO 8601 format.

topic_id.description: The topic ID to scope the email to. If the recipient is a contact and opted-in to the topic, the email is sent. If opted-out, the email is not sent. If the recipient is not a contact, the email is sent if the topic's default subscription is opt_in.

path.description: Path where the attachment file is hosted

content_type.description: Optional content type for the attachment, if not set it will be derived from the filename property

content_id.description: Content ID for embedding inline images using cid references (e.g., cid:image001).

name.description: The name of the email tag. It can only contain ASCII letters (a–z, A–Z), numbers (0–9), underscores (_), or dashes (-). It can contain no more than 256 characters.

value.description: The value of the email tag.It can only contain ASCII letters (a–z, A–Z), numbers (0–9), underscores (_), or dashes (-). It can contain no more than 256 characters.

id.description: The id of the published email template.

variables.description: Template variables object with key/value pairs.

id.description: The ID of the sent email.

scheduled_at.description: Schedule email to be sent later. The date should be in ISO 8601 format.

items.description: The email addresses of the recipients.

from.description: The email address of the sender.

created_at.description: The date and time the email was created.

subject.description: The subject line of the email.

html.description: The HTML body of the email.

text.description: The plain text body of the email.

bcc.description: The email addresses of the blind carbon copy recipients.

cc.description: The email addresses of the carbon copy recipients.

reply_to.description: The email addresses to which replies should be sent.

has_more.description: Indicates if there are more results available.

id.description: The ID of the sent email.

sending.description: Enable or disable sending emails from this domain.

receiving.description: Enable or disable receiving emails to this domain.

DomainCapabilities.description: Configure the domain capabilities for sending and receiving emails. At least one capability must be enabled.

name.description: The name of the domain you want to create.

region.description: The region where emails will be sent from. Possible values are us-east-1 | eu-west-1 | sa-east-1 | ap-northeast-1

custom_return_path.description: For advanced use cases, choose a subdomain for the Return-Path address. Defaults to 'send' (i.e., send.yourdomain.tld).

open_tracking.description: Track the open rate of each email.

click_tracking.description: Track clicks within the body of each HTML email.

tls.description: TLS mode. Opportunistic attempts secure connection but falls back to unencrypted. Enforced requires TLS or email won't be sent.

tracking_subdomain.description: The subdomain to use for click and open tracking.

created_at.description: The date and time the domain was created.

status.description: The status of the domain.

region.description: The region where the domain is hosted.

open_tracking.description: Whether open tracking is enabled for this domain.

click_tracking.description: Whether click tracking is enabled for this domain.

tracking_subdomain.description: The subdomain used for click and open tracking.

open_tracking.description: Track the open rate of each email.

click_tracking.description: Track clicks within the body of each HTML email.

tracking_subdomain.description: The subdomain to use for click and open tracking.

record.description: The type of record (SPF for sending, DKIM for sending, Receiving for inbound emails, Tracking & TrackingCAA for click and open tracking).

name.description: The name of the DNS record.

ttl.description: The time to live for the record.

status.description: The status of the record.

priority.description: The priority of the record (only applicable for MX records).

permission.description: The API key can have full access to Resend’s API or be only restricted to send emails. * full_access - Can create, delete, get, and update any resource. * sending_access - Can only send emails.

domain_id.description: Restrict an API key to send emails only from a specific domain. Only used when the permission is sending_access.

token.description: The token of the API key.

key.description: A unique key for this step within the automation graph.

config.description:
"Configuration for the step. Shape depends on `type`: - **trigger**: `{ event_name: string }` - **send_email**: `{ template: { id: string, variables?: object }, subject?: string, from?: string, reply_to?: string }` - **delay**: `{ duration: string }` — a human-readable duration (e.g. `\"30 minutes\"`) - **wait_for_event**: `{ event_name: string, timeout?: string, filter_rule?: object }` — `timeout` is a human-readable duration (e.g. `\"1 hour\"`) - **condition**: A rule tree with `type` (`rule`, `and`, `or`), `field`, `operator`, and `value` - **contact_update**: `{ first_name?: string|object, last_name?: string|object, unsubscribed?: boolean|object, properties?: object }` - **contact_delete**: `{}` - **add_to_segment**: `{ segment_id: string }`
"

AutomationStep.description: A step in an automation workflow. The `config` object varies based on the step `type`.

key.description: The unique key of this step within the automation graph.

config.description:
"Configuration for the step. Shape depends on `type`. For `delay` steps, config contains `{ duration: string }` with a human-readable duration (e.g. `\"30 minutes\"`). For `wait_for_event` steps, config contains `{ event_name: string, timeout?: string, filter_rule?: object }` where `timeout` is a human-readable duration.
"

AutomationStepResponse.description: A step as returned when retrieving an automation.

type.description: The type of connection. Defaults to `default`.

AutomationConnection.description: A connection between two steps in the automation graph.

status.description: The initial status of the automation. Defaults to `disabled`.

steps.description: The steps that compose the automation workflow. Must include at least one `trigger` step.

connections.description: The connections between steps in the automation graph.

status.description: The current status of the automation.

connections.description: The connections between steps in the active version of the automation.

updated_at.description: The date and time the automation was last updated.

has_more.description: Indicates if there are more results available.

status.description: The status of the automation.

steps.description: The steps that compose the automation workflow. Must be provided together with `connections`.

connections.description: The connections between steps in the automation graph. Must be provided together with `steps`.

PatchAutomationRequest.description: At least one of `name`, `status`, or `steps` and `connections` must be provided. When updating the workflow graph, both `steps` and `connections` must be provided together.

schema.description: A flat key/type map defining the event payload schema. Supported types are `string`, `number`, `boolean`, and `date`.

updated_at.description: The date and time the event was last updated.

name.description: The name of the event. Cannot start with the reserved `resend:` prefix.

id.description: The ID of the updated event.
The ID of the deleted event.

deleted.description: Indicates whether the event was deleted.

event.description: The name of the event to send.

contact_id.description: The ID of the contact to associate with this event. Exactly one of `contact_id` or `email` must be provided.

email.description: The email address to associate with this event. Exactly one of `contact_id` or `email` must be provided.

payload.description: An optional payload of key/value pairs to include with the event.

event.description: The name of the event that was sent.

diagnosticCode.description: Array of SMTP diagnostic responses from the receiving server, one entry per recipient (e.g., `smtp; 550 5.1.1 user unknown`).

message.description: Detailed bounce message describing why the email bounced.

ipAddress.description: IP address of the user who clicked the link.

userAgent.description: User agent string of the browser that clicked the link.

message.description: Detailed suppression message describing why the email was suppressed.

content_type.description: The MIME type of the attachment.

content_id.description: Content-ID header used for referencing inline images.

record.description: The purpose of the DNS record (SPF and DKIM for sending; Receiving MX for inbound emails; Tracking and TrackingCAA for click and open tracking).

value.description: DNS record value to be set.

ttl.description: Time to live for the DNS record (e.g., `Auto`, `60`).

status.description: Verification status of this specific record.

priority.description: Priority value (only applicable for MX records).

from.description: Sender email address and name in the format `Name <email@domain.com>`.

to.description: Array of impacted recipient email addresses.

broadcast_id.description: Unique identifier for the broadcast campaign, if applicable.

template_id.description: Unique identifier for the template used, if applicable.

tags.description: Tag key-value pairs associated with the email.

headers.description: Custom headers included with the email (omitted when no custom headers are set).
